{{short description|American academic}}
{{Infobox scientist
| name              = Bernd Herbert Schmitt
| birth_date        = {{birth date and age|1957|10|22}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Bernd Schmitt |url=https://entities.oclc.org/worldcat/entity/E39PBJpDTdMCJpdFYTqqcVvrbd.html |publisher=[[WorldCat]] |access-date=26 December 2025}}</ref>
| birth_place       = [[Heidelberg]], [[Germany]]
| field             = [[Marketing]]
| workplaces        = [[Columbia Business School]], New York City
| alma_mater        = {{unbulleted list|[[University of Heidelberg]]|[[Cornell University]]}}
| thesis_title      = Functional orientations toward advertisements and consumer products
| thesis_url        = https://catalog.library.cornell.edu/catalog/1602383
| thesis_year       = 1988
| doctoral_advisor  = [[Daryl Bem]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Cornell Alumni News |url=https://ecommons.cornell.edu/server/api/core/bitstreams/da6b0a7e-e45c-44cc-9e9b-2d8fb1298890/content |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240623012754/https://ecommons.cornell.edu/server/api/core/bitstreams/da6b0a7e-e45c-44cc-9e9b-2d8fb1298890/content |archive-date=23 June 2024 |page=29 |date=July 1983}}</ref>
| website           = {{URL|http://www.meetschmitt.com}}
}}
'''Bernd Herbert Schmitt''' is an American marketing researcher. He is the Robert D. Calkins Professor of International Business at [[Columbia Business School]] in New York City. Schmitt works in the fields of [[brand management]] and [[customer experience]].

== Biography ==
Schmitt was born in [[Heidelberg]], Germany,<ref>{{cite news |last1=Boller |first1=Andreas |title=So funktioniert Marketing morgen |url=https://www.wz.de/nrw/wuppertal/marketingexperte-aus-new-york-doziert-an-der-bergischen-universitaet_aid-37878001 |access-date=24 December 2025 |work=[[Westdeutsche Zeitung]] |date=4 April 2019 |language=de}}</ref> and attended the [[University of Heidelberg]], from which he earned a Master’s degree in psychology in 1984. Four years later, he received a PhD in psychology from [[Cornell University]]. Schmitt joined [[Columbia University]] in 1988 and became professor in 1998. A year later, he founded the Center on Global Brand Leadership, serving as its first director. In 2003, he was named the Robert D. Calkins Professor of International Business.<ref name="cv">{{cite web |title=Schmitt, Bernd Vitae June 2024 |url=https://business.columbia.edu/sites/default/files-efs/person/cv/Schmitt%2C%20Bernd%20Vitae%20June%202024%20.pdf |publisher=[[Columbia Business School]] |access-date=24 December 2025 |date=June 2024}}</ref>

Schmitt has been researching and teaching in Asia since the early 1990s.<ref name="st"/> In 1996, he was appointed head of Marketing at the [[China Europe International Business School]], the first marketing chair in Asia, a position he held until 2000.<ref name="cv"/><ref>{{cite book |last1=Croll |first1=Elisabeth |title=China's New Consumers: Social Development and Domestic Demand |date=2006 |publisher=[[Taylor & Francis]] |isbn=978-1134220533 |quote=The article quotes Bernd Schmitt, an expert in consumer behaviour from Columbia Business School in New York and China's first professor of marketing}}</ref> Later, in 2011, he began a two-year term in Singapore to lead the newly created Institute on Asian Consumer Insight at [[Nanyang Technological University]].<ref name="st">{{cite news |last1=Lim |first1=Jessica |title=Buying into branding: Asian firms focus on products and neglect customer experience: Marketing guru |work=[[The Straits Times]] |date=14 December 2011}}</ref>

Schmitt studies marketing, brand management, and customer experience. He has authored nine books translated into more than 30 languages,<ref>{{cite web |last1=Blass |first1=Uwe |title=Wuppertal... and the untapped innovation potential of an exciting city |url=https://www.uni-wuppertal.de/en/transfer/science-communication/transfer-stories/transfer-stories-2019/prof-dr-bernd-schmitt/ |publisher=[[University of Wuppertal]] |access-date=24 December 2025 |date=10 July 2019}}</ref> in one of these, he became the first to introduce the term "[[experiential marketing]]".<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Srivastava |first1=Rajesh Kumar |title=How experiential marketing can be used to build brands - a case study of two specialty stores |journal=[[Innovative Marketing]] |date=2008 |volume=4 |issue=2 |page=70 |url=https://www.businessperspectives.org/images/pdf/applications/publishing/templates/article/assets/2234/IM_en_2008_2_Srivastava.pdf}}</ref> He has served on the marketing boards of [[Volkswagen]] and [[Samsung]] and was the CEO of a small consulting firm. From 2021 to 2024, Schmitt served as the editor-in-chief of the ''[[Journal of Consumer Research]]''.<ref name="cv"/>

== Publications ==

</ref>

*{{cite book |last1=Schmitt |first1=Bernd H. |last2=Simonson | author1-mask=1|first2=Alex |title=Marketing Aesthetics: The Strategic Management of Brands, Identity and Image |publisher=[[Free Press (publisher)|Free Press]] |isbn=978-0684826554|date=1997}}<ref>Review of ''Marketing Aesthetics'':

</ref>
{{refend}}
